codeReview.summary: The feature implementation matches the compile-guard requirements: Store derive now rejects secure+unique combinations in either attribute order, excludes secure fields from fallback lookup metadata, and emits a compile-time error when no Id or legal non-secure fallback lookup path remains. Regression coverage preserves the prior Sensitive and Store diagnostics and adds the requested metadata-level assertion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still instructs workers to run a failing-test-first workflow and GitNexus detect_changes, but the reviewed worker could not follow those steps exactly on this Windows mission because .factory/init.sh is POSIX-shell-only and the installed GitNexus CLI lacked the documented detect_changes command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations for session b7e06aa8-cc22-47a3-87b1-88ceaf0c471d; mission AGENTS.md notes GitNexus tooling is unavailable in worker sessions; skill file C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md still assumes the procedure is directly executable.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state documents the secure-lookup rule and general architecture, but does not capture that the Store derive now accepts the secure helper attribute directly and that fallback lookup metadata should be asserted through UniqueLookupMeta::lookup_fields() in integration tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Implementation in macros/src/lib.rs adds #[proc_macro_derive(Store, attributes(unique, secure))] plus secure-field filtering for lookup_fields; coverage in core/tests/integration_db.rs uses ItSensitiveFallbackLookup::lookup_fields() to assert [alias, note]; .factory/library/architecture.md mentions the rule at a high level but not the concrete testing seam.

summary: Reviewed commit e27f9d51b554052575161ee65c4a3e3ac63108ac for secure-lookup compile guards. The macro changes and added tests satisfy the feature description and validation contract, and I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues.
